title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Changed-Case Lab
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a tested prompt template with diverse examples with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.
Include counterexamples and near misses when category boundaries are material.
Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Template Versioning applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data.
